name: Peñaflor to Grado on the Camino Primitivo
name_original: Peñaflor a Grado en el Camino Primitivo
region_intro: Hiking around Candamo offers diverse landscapes, from lush forests to scenic river valleys, providing varied terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features a network of trails, including sections of the historic Camino Primitivo, that traverse hills and ancient cattle tracks. These paths showcase the natural beauty and historical heritage of Asturias. The area's topography includes gentle elevations suitable for all fitness levels, alongside more demanding treks. Best hiking trails around Candamo The most popular hiking route is Peñaflor to Grado on the Camino Primitivo, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic pilgrimage path with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Grado – Camino Primitivo Countryside loop from Castañéu, a difficult 18.3 miles (29.4 km) path. This challenging loop offers extensive views of the countryside and significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the View of Grado – Casita Mandala loop from Cabruñana, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Candamo is defined by lush forests, river valleys, and ancient cattle tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle strolls to challenging treks. The routes in Candamo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Candamo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Candamo?
A: Candamo offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 100 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to challenging treks, ensuring options for all fitness levels. Specifically, there are around 35 easy routes, 69 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Candamo's hiking trails?
A: The hiking trails in Candamo feature diverse terrain, characterized by lush forests, scenic river valleys, and ancient cattle tracks. You'll find paths winding through indigenous forests of chestnut, hazelnut, and oak trees, as well as routes along the River Nalon where original Roman pavements can still be seen. The region offers both gentle elevations and more demanding ascents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Candamo?
A: Yes, Candamo has several trails suitable for families. Many routes are designed for minimal elevation gain and can be completed in under two hours, making them ideal for all ages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31908232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villa Paladín Albergue – Peñaflor Bridge loop from Grado&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11 km route with relatively flat terrain, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Candamo?
A: Most trails in Candamo are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near roads, and to respect local regulations regarding pets. The region's varied landscapes provide plenty of open spaces for dogs to explore.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Candamo?
A: Yes, Candamo offers several circular hiking routes that allow you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Primitivo Countryside – Grado Town Center loop from Cabruñana&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.3 km trail that offers a pleasant loop through the countryside. The region also features local trails like the Camín de Piñera, a mostly flat circular route through Grullos.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while hiking in Candamo?
A: Candamo is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. Hikers can discover ancient Roman pavements along the Camin Real de la Mesa, explore indigenous forests, and enjoy views of the River Nalon. Significant historical sites include the UNESCO-recognized La Peña Cave paintings, and the 17th-century Palace of Valdes Bazan. You can also visit nearby attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1137104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte de Galiñeiro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Candamo?
A: The best time to visit Candamo for hiking is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant with blooming flora or autumn colors. The region is also known for its Strawberry Festival in early June, adding a unique local experience.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic overlooks along the trails?
A: Yes, Candamo's varied topography offers several opportunities for scenic views. Many trails provide extensive views of the countryside and river valleys. You can find dedicated viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peñona&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Lliberra – View of the Meanders of the Nora River&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Candamo?
A: The hiking trails in Candamo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from lush forests to river valleys, and appreciate the well-maintained paths that showcase both natural beauty and historical heritage. Over 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore the area.

Q: Are there options for challenging hikes in Candamo?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Candamo offers several demanding routes. These trails often feature significant elevation gain and cover longer distances, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40324956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Grado – Casita Mandala loop from Cabruñana&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 17.2 km trail with considerable ascent.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Candamo?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead can vary, Candamo is conveniently located less than 30 minutes from the city of Oviedo, which serves as a regional transport hub. Some sections of the Camino Primitivo, which passes through Candamo, are accessible via local bus services connecting towns like Peñaflor and Grado. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific routes and stops.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ancient ruins to discover on Candamo's trails?
A: Absolutely. Candamo is rich in history. You can hike along sections of the ancient Camino Primitivo, part of the historic Route of Santiago, and even find original Roman pavements along the Camin Real de la Mesa. The region also features fortifications and bunkers from the Spanish Civil War in the mountains of El Pedrosu and Bufaran, offering a glimpse into more recent history.
tips:
Grado exudes charm with its picturesque streets, historic architecture, and vibrant atmosphere. Nestled amidst lush greenery and surrounded by mountains, Grado offers a perfect blend of natural beauty and cultural richness. 

Visitors can wander through its narrow cobblestone streets, explore medieval churches, and indulge in delicious local cuisine at charming cafes and restaurants. With its warm hospitality and authentic Spanish ambiance.
From this small watchtower you can see Grado, council of Asturias. What is most striking, however, is the greenery of the vegetation.

The Peñaflor Bridge, also known as the Medieval Peñaflor Bridge, is an artistic work that combines antiquity and elegant solidity. It was built in the 12th century and has been rebuilt several times due to floods.
